Tony Robbins: Hey, it's Tony Robbins again. We're about to begin our program now, and I
really want to start out by, first, again, acknowledging and thanking you for
joining us, because everybody talks a good game. Everybody talks about they
want to change their life. They want to build a business, but how many people
really do? I'm not sure why you got started with us, here. Why you made this
investment in yourself, and in your future business, but I don't if it's because
you're looking for a part-time income, or there's some expertise inside of you
that you just want to bring to the world, regardless of economics, or if this is
your full-time primary focus. You want to build something that you're going to
build your life around, like I have.
Whatever your reason is, we're going to give you the very best tools, but we
also talk straight. And to talk straight, that's the only way I know how to talk, is
most people are not going to succeed. And if you're going to be the exception,
not the rule, you got to understand what stops people from succeeding? Most
people don't live the life of their dreams. You know and I know it. Most people
are in North America are not fit and healthy. They are not in the relationship
they want, whether it's love and passion sustained over decades. Most people
are not earning what they want to earn. Most people are not as happy as they
really want to be. So, if that's true, that sounds depressing to some people. I'm
no idiot. I don't have delusions. I'm not some positive thinker. I know most
people don't succeed, but a few do.
I've been obsessed my entire life with a few who do versus the many who talk,
versus the many who have excuses, versus the many who have reasons why it
won't workout. They might be legitimate reasons, but reasons aren't going to
get you where you want to go. Results come from an obsession with getting
yourself to take action. You and I both know, so many people talk about things,
and they get excited about things, but why is it that people have all the
resources to succeed fail? I'm sure you've known somebody in your life who had
great family support, lots of love, tremendous education, great economic
opportunities, and they spent their life going in and out of rehab.
Then there are other people that life has kicked in the face, that life has been,
seemingly, massively unjust. They've been abused mentally, emotionally,
physically, any way. And many times, those are the very individuals that are the
people we all know about. The people that shift our culture. The Oprahs of the
world, so to speak. It has nothing to do with biography. It has nothing to do with
background. It has nothing to do with resources. Resources are not the
problem. The problem is people are not resourceful enough.
In this very first session, before we get into the content, you and I have to get
really clear what does it take to succeed? And the answer is massive, relentless
action. If anybody who can constantly take action, and then adjust their
approach if something doesn't work, change to something else. Try it again. Try
something slightly different. Those are the individuals you and I both know
succeed. It doesn't matter where it is. Listen, most businesses do not succeed.

You look at businesses today, in the first year 50% of all businesses are gone. In
five years, 80% are no longer exist. Over a 10-year period, 96% of businesses
fail. That means 4% make it. That doesn't mean they're profitable. It just means
they're still standing.
If you're saying, "I'm going to get into this new industry and I'm going to
succeed." It going to require a different level of commitment. What's going to
get you to take action when you're feeling fearful? When you're feeling
uncertain? When it's new? That's what you've got to deal with, even before you
get to the skills. Because, otherwise, the skills will be there, but you won't
execute. How many people do you know that know what to do, but they don't
do what they know? I'm sure you've done in the past, and so did I. I'm not here
to tell you this. I have some super human person who had this unbelievable
willpower. What I had was mission.
I think that's what you got to decide right up front. Why am I doing this?
Reasons come first, answers come second brother and sister. You got to
understand, Jim Roden used to teach me this. He said, "Tony, if you get strong
enough why to do something, you can figure out how to do anything." But you
got to know what that mission is, and I found, in my life, if it's just to make
money, it's not enough. Don't get me wrong, we want to show you how to make

eminence and there are people, just like you, that have their financial freedom,
because they've learned how to bring knowledge to the marketplace that
changes the quality of people's businesses and their lives.
But in order to get yourself to follow through, you're going to have to have
something even more than money. It's really got be something in your core. You
want to deliver something. I've always found that human beings will do more
others that we care about than we'll ever do for ourselves. So, if you can figure
out something, why are you doing this? What's the reason? That will get you to
take action when the tough times come, but you need ... Along with mission,
you need a tool, and it's the tool of momentum. This single tool, I come back to

failure. It's the difference between sustained success and someone who starts
out for a little while, and they dribble away.
I'm in an industry where I've seen so many people come and go over the
decades, and here I am, 42 years later, and they difference is the ability to
understand momentum. What is momentum? Well, I'm sure you've seen, at
times, a sports team, maybe an NBA basketball game, or some sporting event,
and one team is ... they seem to be dominating and then something happens.
There's a moment when the certainty in the other team shifts. Where, all the
sudden, their hunger gets ignited, or somebody makes a play, or somebody
dives, or somebody does something courageous, and inspires people. There's an
energy that shifts. And, all the sudden, boom, the game changes.

Tony Robbins: ... in public. People see somebody so successful like I said before and they look

been a certain income, and it seemed virtually impossible when you thought of

you notice. I don't know if you've ever done this, if you've ever, ladies, bought a
certain outfit, or ladies or gentlemen, bought a certain car. You see that outfit or
car everywhere. Well were those cars and outfits around before? Of course, but
now that you own one your RAS, your reticular activating system says that's
important, and so it sees it everywhere.
